programmes.show_description: Crazy, Stupid, Lump. Jess is looking for love but has a giant bump on his neck. Former NFL defensive tackle, Marcell Dareus, hopes Dr. Lee can tackle his forehead lipoma. S5, Ep18
California Bumpin'. Brent has a tricep bump that could be intertwined in his muscle. Plus, Jeb avoids the beach because of the massive number of bumps all over his body. S5, Ep19
Bettie Jo's Story. At just 24, Bettie Jo is eating her way towards 700-lbs. Housebound, she is reliant on her husband for even basic needs. But can she shed the pounds? S3, Ep4
Joe's Story. Joe is only 30, and trapped in an 800-lb body. He lost his job and is mostly housebound. Can Joe find the strength to face his past and save his life? S3, Ep5
Thank You, Next. ICU nurse Isabella is hoping her final look will come together just days before her wedding. Plus, LaQuesha is determined to find two blingy dresses! S19, Ep8
Mom, You've Said Just Enough. Christie loves plunging necklines, but her mum doesn't want anything too revealing. Monique is so picky that she has Mindy and Randy both stumped. S19, Ep9
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Cops On The Rocks. Mike and Carl decide to go to couples counselling to try and reconcile their partnership. Plus, Molly decides to do a promotional radio interview. S6, Ep1
One Small Step For Mike. Molly has high hopes that a fitness tracking device will encourage Mike to be more active. But Mike has other plans for it. S6, Ep2
Peg O' My Heart Attack. After Peg suffers a heart attack after a heated argument with Molly, Mike blames his wife, who immediately sets out to make amends. S6, Ep3
Spock, Kirk And Testicular Hernia. George Sr. makes a deal with Sheldon to tutor his brother when Georgie's poor grades threaten his place on the football team. S1, Ep9
Eagle Feather, String Bean. Sheldon interviews with a school for gifted children. Much to their surprise, the rest of the family struggle with his absence. S1, Ep10
Demons, Sunday School. A worried Mary sends Sheldon to Sunday school after she finds him playing Dungeons & Dragons with Tam and Billy. S1, Ep11
The Friendship Algorithim. Looking to get friendly with Barry Kripke to gain access to a piece of high-tech lab equipment, Sheldon develops a flowchart process to make friends. S2, Ep13
The Financial Permeability. When Sheldon loans money to Penny so she can pay her rent, it leads to Leonard coming face-to-face with her ex, Kurt. S2, Ep14
The Maternal Capacitance. A visit from Leonard's mother ends up bringing him and Penny closer. S2, Ep15
The Cushion Saturation. Paintball leads to a squabble between Sheldon and Penny as well as romance between Howard and Leslie. S2, Ep16
The Terminator Decoupling. The gang is thrilled to see Summer Glau is also aboard their train to San Francisco. Sheldon frets over having forgotten a paper he wants to present. S2, Ep17
The Work Song Nanocluster. Penny's business selling hair accessories out of her apartment gets out of hand when an over-caffeinated Sheldon takes charge. S2, Ep18
The Dead Hooker Juxtaposition. Penny feels threatened when another sexy woman moves into the apartment building and angered when she senses the guys are being taken advantage of. S2, Ep19
The Hofstadter Isotope. A fellow comic book enthusiast's hitting on Penny leads to Leonard, Raj and Howard going to a bar's Ladies' Night. S2, Ep20
Super Cop. Mike becomes hyper-vigilant after his wallet is stolen from the squad car while taking a nap. S6, Ep4
Joyce's Will Be Done. Molly is shocked when Joyce reveals she's leaving the house to Victoria in her will. S6, Ep5
The Good Wife. Molly's leisure activities between novels become an issue for Mike. Tensions rise as he wants her to be the perfect homemaker. S6, Ep6
A Computer, A Plastic Pony. Sheldon asking for a computer has unforeseen consequences for his parents, prompting Mary and the twins to move in with Meemaw. S1, Ep12
A Sneeze, Detention. Sheldon's germophobia lands him in detention when flu season hits Medford and he takes extreme measures to remain healthy. S1, Ep13
Potato Salad, A Broomstick. When Mary takes a job at the church, Sheldon and Missy are left home alone for the very first time. S1, Ep14
The Vegas Renormalization. Leonard and Raj take Wolowitz to Las Vegas to help him get over his break-up. Sheldon locks himself out and has only one other place to stay: Penny's. S2, Ep21
Classified Material Turbulence. Stuart asks Leonard's advice for a date with Penny. Wolowitz realises there's an error in a toilet he designed for the International Space Station. S2, Ep22
The Monopolar Expedition. The guys receive a prestigious offer to work over the summer at the North Pole, and Penny's resultant behaviour leaves Leonard unsure if he should go. S2, Ep23
The Electric Can Opener. Discovering Leonard, Raj and Howard fudged the results of his Arctic research expedition, a disgraced Sheldon heads home to Texas. S3, Ep1
The Jiminy Conjecture. Sheldon and Howard wager comic books over a cricket's species. Meanwhile, a less-than-perfect romantic night agonizes Leonard and Penny. S3, Ep2
The Gothowitz Deviation. Howard takes Raj along to check out the Goth dating scene. Sheldon uses chocolate as part of a behaviour modification experiment on Penny. S3, Ep3
The Pirate Solution. Facing a return to India, Raj needs a job desperately enough to consider working for Sheldon. Howard becomes a third wheel to Leonard and Penny. S3, Ep4
Creepy Candy Coating Corollary. Sheldon looks forward to squaring off against a Star Trek actor in a fantasy card game tournament. Wolowitz cashes in on an old pact with Leonard. S3, Ep5
Please Remain Seated. Forrest and Sheena's families meet. Jovon and Annalynn don't see eye to eye. Laura grows suspicious of Birkan. Rick is unsure about having more kids. S8, Ep8
McAllister Auto Loves The Ladies. Jim feels ganged up on when Georgie and Audrey make changes to the tire store, and Mandy helps Georgie navigate a fight between her parents. S1, Ep13
A Sportsbook And A Breakup. Mandy finds out Meemaw runs an illegal sportsbook and wants in on the action. Meanwhile, Georgie struggles to keep Mandy's new job a secret. S1, Ep14
Quadripple Nipple. Don has a case of crippling psoriasis that has completely taken over his body. Jeff has a huge growth over his spine that looks like a hunchback. S5, Ep20
Dr. Pimple Popper. Angelina's huge chest lump affects her love life and Delano has a football-sized mass on his back. Plus, Carla hopes to get painful cysts removed from her scalp. S1, Ep1
Angel's Story. At 42 years old, Angel is staring death in the face. She is struggling to make a positive change and be an active part of her partner and son's lives. S3, Ep6
Charity's Story. Suffering from flesh-eating bacteria and weighing almost 800-lbs, Charity relies on her family. When bad news threatens her weight loss can she carry on? S3, Ep7
Kleinfeld, Here I Come! Morgan worries her mum will reject her dress three months before her wedding. Elaina must take back control of her appointment from her bossy sister. S19, Ep10
A Season of Surprises. Brides have high expectations and occasionally there are shocking moments in the salon. Randy looks back at some of the best moments this season. S19, Ep11
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A Clogged Pore. Sheldon struggles with the first signs of puberty while his parents hunt for new jobs, and Georgie and Meemaw take a business trip across the border. S5, Ep22
Pilot. Nine-year-old Sheldon learns that his high intelligence isn't always helpful growing up in East Texas, where church and football are king. S1, Ep1
Rockets, Dewey Decimal System. To appease his worried mother, Sheldon employs the techniques of a self-help book to try and make a friend. S1, Ep2
Poker, Faith And Eggs. When George Sr. is rushed to the emergency room, Meemaw comes to babysit. And the kids have an adventure getting to the hospital on their own. S1, Ep3
A Therapist, A Comic Book. Sheldon refuses to eat solid food after a choking scare but finds solace in a new interest after he discovers comic books. S1, Ep4
A Solar Calculator, A Game Ball. Sheldon uses his statistics skills to help his father coach the football team. Both Sheldon and Georgie struggle with the popularity this brings. S1, Ep5
McAllister Auto Loves The Ladies. Jim feels ganged up on when Georgie and Audrey make changes to the tire store, and Mandy helps Georgie navigate a fight between her parents. S1, Ep13
A Sportsbook And A Breakup. Mandy finds out Meemaw runs an illegal sportsbook and wants in on the action. Meanwhile, Georgie struggles to keep Mandy's new job a secret. S1, Ep14
The Middle Earth Paradigm. Penny hosts a Halloween party, putting Leonard, Sheldon, Wolowitz and Koothrappali through a new test of their social skills. S1, Ep6
The Dumpling Paradox. When Wolowitz goes AWOL with one of her friends, Penny's recruited to fill in for him on the guys' Halo team. S1, Ep7
The Grasshopper Experiment. Raj's parents set him up on a blind date, and when he discovers he can talk to her, she shows interest in Sheldon. S1, Ep8
Cooper-Hofstadter Polarization. When they're invited to present at a physics conference, Sheldon's reluctance to share the credit leads to a tiff between him and Leonard. S1, Ep9
The Loobenfeld Decay. Leonard and Sheldon each lie to avoid hearing Penny sing in a production of Rent. However, Sheldon's lie becomes a bit too complicated. S1, Ep10
The Pancake Batter Anomaly. With his friends knowing how sour he acts when sick and avoiding him as a result, Sheldon is nursed by Penny, who hasn't a clue what she's in for. S1, Ep11
The Jerusalem Duality. With Sheldon rattled after meeting Dennis, a teen physicist who's smarter than he is, the rest of the guys search for a way to eliminate the threat. S1, Ep12
The Bat Jar Conjecture. Sheldon finds himself booted from the gang's Physics Bowl team and replaced with Leslie, so he forms his own team hoping to prove he's superior. S1, Ep13
The Nerdvana Annihilation. When the delivery of a full-size prop of 1960's 'The Time Machine' causes Penny to miss work, she scolds Leonard about his sci-fi collectibles. S1, Ep14
The Pork Chop Indeterminacy. The gang fall for Sheldon's twin sister, and Leonard's plan to win her heart is derailed when Sheldon declares he'll decide who can date her. S1, Ep15
The Peanut Reaction. Penny wants to throw a surprise party for Leonard, but Sheldon's indulgence at an electronics store throws her off-track. S1, Ep16
The Tangerine Factor. Penny's breakup spurs Leonard to summon the courage to ask her out, and they both go to Sheldon for advice on their upcoming date. S1, Ep17
The Bad Fish Paradigm. Penny opens up to Sheldon after a less-than-stellar first date with Leonard, placing him in the uncomfortable position of having to keep a secret. S2, Ep1
The Codpiece Topology. Seeing Penny with another guy sends Leonard back to Leslie Winkle, much to Sheldon's dismay. S2, Ep2
The Barbarian Sublimation. Penny becomes addicted to an online game based on the world of Conan the Barbarian and persistently pesters Sheldon for advice. S2, Ep3
The Griffin Equivalency. Full of confidence following being named one of People's 30 to Watch Under 30, Raj hits on Penny and becomes impossibly arrogant. S2, Ep4
The Euclid Alternative. An intervention is used to try to force Sheldon to overcome his fear of driving. S2, Ep5
The Cooper-Nowitzki Theorem. Sheldon and his friends are mystified when a younger undergraduate student is attracted to him. S2, Ep6
The Panty Pinata Polarization. When Sheldon bans Penny from the apartment, she decides to retaliate. Howard and Raj search for the America's Next Top Model house. S2, Ep7
The Lizard-Spock Expansion. Sheldon wants to use an alternate version of rock-paper-scissors to help resolve conflicts. A girl Wolowitz has a major crush on falls for Leonard. S2, Ep8
Dara Ó Briain introduces topical chat and laugh out loud games with guests Rhys James, Ed Byrne, Glenn Moore, Sara Pascoe and Emmanuel Sonubi. S1, Ep5
Soon-to-be-married Peter's lack of male friends prompts him to undertake a quest to find true 'bromance' so he can have a best man at the wedding.
